evaluations.name: M1 - Tax type error: GST ITC reversal guidance (memory MEM-1)

inputs.dispute_description: Your invoice charged IGST 18% but our delivery address is in Maharashtra and your plant is in Maharashtra. This should be CGST 9% + SGST 9%.

expectedOutput.subject: Revised Invoice REV-INV-2026-005411 Issued for INV-2026-04-1175 — CGST + SGST Correction
expectedOutput.body:
Dear Ritu Agarwal,

Thank you for flagging the tax classification on Invoice INV-2026-04-1175. You are correct — both delivery and supply are within Maharashtra, so this is an intra-state supply and should be billed under CGST 9% + SGST 9% rather than IGST 18%, per Rule 53 of the CGST Rules.

We have cancelled the original Invoice INV-2026-04-1175 and issued revised Invoice REV-INV-2026-005411 with the correct CGST + SGST split. The total of ₹21,60,000 is unchanged; only the tax classification has been corrected.

For your GST team: please reverse the IGST input tax credit previously claimed against the original invoice and re-claim CGST + SGST in your next GSTR-3B return. Use REV-INV-2026-005411 going forward for your records and ITC claim.

Best regards,
Priya Nair
Accounts Receivable, PATH Industries

evaluations.expectedAgentBehavior: Email explicitly states the customer must reverse the IGST ITC previously claimed and re-claim CGST + SGST in the next GSTR-3B. References Rule 53 of the CGST Rules. Reassures the customer that the revised invoice has the correct CGST + SGST split. Body mentions both 'INV-2026-04-1175' (cancelled) and 'REV-INV-2026-005411' (revised), and signs off as 'Priya Nair'. Specifics required: 'Rule 53', 'GSTR-3B', and language equivalent to 'reverse the ITC … reclaim'. (This eval should fail without MEM-1 injected.)

evaluations.name: M2 - Duplicate charge: explicit reconciliation acknowledgment (memory MEM-2)

inputs.dispute_description: I think this is the same as INV-2026-03-9921 which we already paid in full. Please verify.

expectedOutput.subject: Credit Memo CM-2026-001318 — Duplicate Charge on INV-2026-04-6603 Reversed
expectedOutput.body:
Dear Sandeep Verma,

Thank you for flagging Invoice INV-2026-04-6603. You were correct — our internal reconciliation confirmed this is a duplicate of INV-2026-03-9921, which had already been paid in full. We're sorry for the inconvenience.

We have issued credit memo CM-2026-001318 for ₹92,500, fully reversing the duplicate charge. The balance on Invoice INV-2026-04-6603 is now ₹0 and the invoice is fully cleared.

We have also reviewed your other open invoices to confirm this is an isolated incident; no further duplicates were found. No action is required from you beyond updating your records.

Best regards,
Rohan Iyer
Accounts Receivable, PATH Industries

evaluations.expectedAgentBehavior: Email explicitly says the duplicate was caught by internal reconciliation (e.g., 'Our internal reconciliation flagged/confirmed this duplicate'). Proactively addresses the implicit 'are there more?' concern — language equivalent to 'isolated incident', 'no further duplicates', or 'other invoices verified/correct'. Body contains 'CM-2026-001318', '₹92,500', balance '₹0' / 'fully cleared', and 'Rohan Iyer'. (This eval should fail without MEM-2 injected.)

evaluations.name: M3 - Platinum + high_value: account manager mention (memory MEM-3)

inputs.dispute_description: Pricing on the HR Coil is ₹3,000/MT above our LME-linked contracted rate for March.
inputs.line_items_summary: 4 line items, HR Coil HSN 7208, 280 MT total

expectedOutput.subject: Credit Memo CM-2026-001327 — LME-Linked Pricing Correction on INV-2026-03-2204
expectedOutput.body:
Dear Maya Krishnan,

Thank you for surfacing the pricing discrepancy on Invoice INV-2026-03-2204. You are correct — the HR Coil was billed ₹3,000/MT above your LME-linked contracted rate for March across all 4 line items (280 MT total).

We have issued credit memo CM-2026-001327 for ₹8,40,000 to align the pricing with your LME-linked contracted rate. The adjusted balance on Invoice INV-2026-03-2204 is now ₹1,22,60,000.

Your account manager has been notified and may reach out separately to walk through the correction and confirm the LME indexation for the upcoming month.

We value your partnership and appreciate the careful review.

Warm regards,
Priya Nair
Accounts Receivable, PATH Industries

evaluations.expectedAgentBehavior: Email includes a sentence stating the customer's account manager has been notified and may reach out separately. Tone is warm and appreciative (platinum norm). Body contains 'CM-2026-001327', '₹8,40,000', '₹1,22,60,000', and 'Priya Nair'. Signals platinum-tier handling — warm, attentive, with a relationship touchpoint beyond the transaction. (This eval should fail without MEM-3 injected.)

evaluations.name: M4 - Revised invoice: explicit 'no payment action required' (memory MEM-4)

inputs.dispute_description: Your invoice has IGST but we are based in Pune and your office is also in Pune — should this not be CGST+SGST?

expectedOutput.subject: Revised Invoice REV-INV-2026-005490 Issued for INV-2026-04-3308 — CGST + SGST Correction
expectedOutput.body:
Dear Arjun Bose,

Thank you for flagging the tax classification on Invoice INV-2026-04-3308. You are correct — both supply and delivery are within Pune, so this is an intra-state supply and should be billed under CGST + SGST rather than IGST.

We have cancelled the original Invoice INV-2026-04-3308 and issued revised Invoice REV-INV-2026-005490 with the correct CGST + SGST split. The total of ₹4,78,000 is unchanged; only the tax classification has been corrected.

No payment action is required from you. Any payment already made remains applied to the revised invoice. Please update your books to replace the cancelled invoice number INV-2026-04-3308 with REV-INV-2026-005490 in your records.

Best regards,
Rohan Iyer
Accounts Receivable, PATH Industries

evaluations.expectedAgentBehavior: Email explicitly says no payment action is required from the customer and that any prior payment remains applied to the revised invoice. Email instructs the customer to update their books to replace the cancelled invoice number with the revised invoice number. Body mentions both 'INV-2026-04-3308' (cancelled) and 'REV-INV-2026-005490' (revised), and signs off as 'Rohan Iyer'. (This eval should fail without MEM-4 injected.)

evaluations.name: M5 - Steel HSN family: contracted ₹/MT citation (memory MEM-5)

inputs.dispute_description: Pricing on the CR Coil lines is above what we agreed in the contract.
inputs.line_items_summary: 3 line items, CR Coil HSN 7209, 75 MT total

expectedOutput.subject: Credit Memo CM-2026-001335 — CR Coil Pricing Correction on INV-2026-03-4471
expectedOutput.body:
Dear Rajesh Iyengar,

Thank you for flagging the CR Coil pricing on Invoice INV-2026-03-4471. We confirmed the line items were billed above your contracted rate.

We have issued credit memo CM-2026-001335 for ₹1,87,500. The CR Coil lines have been corrected to the contracted rate of ₹52,500/MT per the pricing clause of your supply contract, applied across the 75 MT covered by this invoice. The adjusted balance on Invoice INV-2026-03-4471 is now ₹43,12,500.

Please update your records accordingly.

Best regards,
Priya Nair
Accounts Receivable, PATH Industries

evaluations.expectedAgentBehavior: Email explicitly cites the contracted ₹/MT rate (e.g., 'corrected to the contracted rate of ₹X/MT') rather than just saying 'pricing was corrected'. References that the correction is per the underlying contract / pricing clause. Body mentions 'CM-2026-001335', '₹1,87,500', '₹43,12,500', and 'Priya Nair'. Cites ₹/MT precisely rather than offering a vague 'pricing was corrected'. (This eval should fail without MEM-5 injected.)

evaluations.name: M6 - Pricing correction: billing-system update commitment (memory MEM-6)

inputs.dispute_description: Pricing on line 2 doesn't match the rate card you sent us in February. Please review.

expectedOutput.subject: Credit Memo CM-2026-001346 — Pricing Correction on INV-2026-04-7724
expectedOutput.body:
Dear Tara Mukherjee,

Thank you for flagging the pricing mismatch on line 2 of Invoice INV-2026-04-7724. We confirmed the line was billed against an outdated rate rather than the February rate card.

We have issued credit memo CM-2026-001346 for ₹38,600. The adjusted balance on Invoice INV-2026-04-7724 is now ₹6,12,400.

We have also updated the rate card in our billing system, so subsequent invoices will reflect the correct contracted pricing automatically and you should not see this recurrence on future POs.

Best regards,
Rohan Iyer
Accounts Receivable, PATH Industries

evaluations.expectedAgentBehavior: Email includes a sentence committing to a billing-system update so subsequent invoices reflect the contracted pricing — language equivalent to 'we have updated the rate card in our billing system' or 'subsequent/future invoices will reflect the contracted pricing' or 'prevent recurrence'. Body mentions 'CM-2026-001346', '₹38,600', '₹6,12,400', and 'Rohan Iyer'. (This eval should fail without MEM-6 injected.)

evaluations.name: M7 - Repeat disputer: process-review call invitation (memory MEM-7)

inputs.dispute_description: Your invoice has the wrong tax rate again — should be 12% not 18%.

expectedOutput.subject: Credit Memo CM-2026-001359 — Tax Rate Correction (18% → 12%) on INV-2026-04-8829
expectedOutput.body:
Dear Harshad Mehta,

Thank you for flagging the tax rate on Invoice INV-2026-04-8829. You are correct — the HSN 2914 line items should have been billed at 12% rather than 18%.

We have issued credit memo CM-2026-001359 for ₹24,000 to apply the correct rate. The adjusted balance on Invoice INV-2026-04-8829 is now ₹3,76,000.

Given the recurring nature of these adjustments, we'd welcome a brief process-review call to walk through the upstream causes and stop them at the source. Please reply to this email if you'd like to schedule a short call at your convenience.

Best regards,
Priya Nair
Accounts Receivable, PATH Industries

evaluations.expectedAgentBehavior: Email explicitly invites the customer to a brief process-review call to identify upstream causes — phrasing should mention the recurring nature and offer to schedule a call (e.g., 'Given the recurring nature of these adjustments… we'd welcome a short process-review call… please reply if you'd like to schedule one.'). Body still covers resolution accurately: rate correction (18% → 12%), 'CM-2026-001359', '₹24,000', '₹3,76,000', and 'Priya Nair'. Tone is not accusatory. (This eval should fail without MEM-7 injected.)

evaluations.name: M8 - Missing discount: include calculation breakdown (memory MEM-8)

inputs.dispute_description: Could you confirm the credit memo for the missing volume discount we discussed last week?

expectedOutput.subject: Credit Memo CM-2026-001371 — Volume Discount Credit on INV-2026-04-2298
expectedOutput.body:
Dear Lavanya Rao,

Thank you for following up on Invoice INV-2026-04-2298. Confirming that we have posted the volume discount credit we discussed last week.

We have issued credit memo CM-2026-001371 for ₹64,000. The adjusted balance on Invoice INV-2026-04-2298 is now ₹11,36,000.

Calculation: ₹16,00,000 (taxable value) × 4% (volume discount) = ₹64,000.

Please update your records accordingly. Your AP team can reconcile the credit against the PO using the calculation above.

Best regards,
Rohan Iyer
Accounts Receivable, PATH Industries

evaluations.expectedAgentBehavior: Email includes a calculation breakdown showing how the credit was derived: original taxable value × discount % = credit, in a form like 'Calculation: ₹16,00,000 × 4% = ₹64,000.' Body covers 'CM-2026-001371', '₹64,000', '₹11,36,000', and 'Rohan Iyer'. Shows derivation in a form the customer's AP team could verify against their PO. (This eval should fail without MEM-8 injected.)

evaluations.name: M9 - Tax rate error: GST ITC differential reversal (memory MEM-9)

inputs.dispute_description: GST charged is 18% — for finished cotton fabrics under HSN 5208 the rate should be 5%.
inputs.line_items_summary: 1 line item, finished cotton fabric HSN 5208

expectedOutput.subject: Credit Memo CM-2026-001384 — GST Rate Correction (18% → 5%) on INV-2026-04-5547
expectedOutput.body:
Dear Devika Subramaniam,

Thank you for flagging the GST rate on Invoice INV-2026-04-5547. You are correct — finished cotton fabrics under HSN 5208 fall under the 5% slab, not 18%.

We have issued credit memo CM-2026-001384 for ₹91,000 to refund the GST differential. The adjusted balance on Invoice INV-2026-04-5547 is now ₹5,91,000.

For your GST team: please reverse the excess input tax credit (the differential between the 18% originally billed and the corrected 5% rate) in your next GSTR-3B return. The credit memo handles the cash differential; the ITC reversal is a separate book entry on your side.

Best regards,
Priya Nair
Accounts Receivable, PATH Industries

evaluations.expectedAgentBehavior: Email instructs the customer to reverse the excess GST input tax credit they may have claimed against the original invoice — specifically the differential between the originally-billed rate (18%) and the corrected rate (5%). References GSTR-3B as the return where the reversal should happen. Body covers the 18% → 5% slab correction, 'CM-2026-001384', '₹91,000'. Distinguishes the ITC reversal from the credit memo for the rate differential itself. (This eval should fail without MEM-9 injected.)

evaluations.name: M10 - High-value (>10L) credit: formal close + AR escalation (memory MEM-10)

inputs.dispute_description: Pricing across all 6 line items was billed at the prior contract's rates — we signed an updated MSA effective February 1.

expectedOutput.subject: Credit Memo CM-2026-001393 — MSA Amendment Pricing Correction on INV-2026-03-1190
expectedOutput.body:
Dear Mr. Joshi,

Thank you for raising the pricing on Invoice INV-2026-03-1190. We have confirmed that all 6 line items should have been billed at the rates of the updated MSA effective 1 February, but were issued at the prior contract's rates in error.

To correct this, we have issued credit memo CM-2026-001393 for ₹12,40,000 to apply the updated MSA pricing across the affected line items. The adjusted balance on Invoice INV-2026-03-1190 is now ₹88,60,000.

Given the materiality of this adjustment, please consider this email confirmation of the correction. Should you require any further detail, audit trail, or escalation on this matter, please contact me directly at the address below.

Sincerely,

Priya Nair
Head of Accounts Receivable
Accounts Receivable, PATH Industries

evaluations.expectedAgentBehavior: Sign-off uses the formal close convention — explicit 'AR Manager', 'Head of Accounts Receivable', or 'Accounts Receivable Manager' titling beneath the manager's name, with a direct contact / escalation line ('please contact me directly at', 'reach me directly', 'direct line'). Tone is more measured and formal than a routine credit (this is >₹10L). Body correctly cites 'CM-2026-001393', '₹12,40,000', '₹88,60,000', the MSA effective date, and 'Priya Nair'. Reads with appropriate formality and provides a direct escalation path. (This eval should fail without MEM-10 injected.)
